db:abstract.db:para: The States example shows how to use the Qt state machine to play animations.

db:article.db:para: The example demonstrates how QStateMachine states can be used to animate properties of widgets. There are three states in the application that can be sequentially triggered by a button. The button initiates state transitions that animate the properties of the application widgets.

db:section.db:title: The main() Function
db:section.db:para: The widgets are created and added to the QGraphicsScene. The Pixmap class is also defined in the example. It extends QGraphicsPixmapItem so that its position can be read and written as Qt properties.
The state machine and the states are created.
Each state is assigned the object properties that they will apply.
The state transitions are created with the trigger of the button click. For each property, a QPropertyAnimation is created that interpolates between the property values.
The state machine is started and the scene is assigned to a GraphicsView.
